info.name: WesTech Products API
info.description:
HTTP REST API for managing products (PHP 8.4, no framework).

## Getting started
1. Start the stack: `docker compose up -d --build` and run migrations: `docker compose exec php php migrate.php`.
2. Set the collection variables (Variables tab):
   - `base_url` — defaults to `http://localhost:8080` (change if `PHP_PORT` differs).
   - `auth_token` — must match `APP_AUTH_TOKEN` from your `.env`.
3. All requests authenticate with a Bearer token (collection-level auth). Authentication is enforced on **every** endpoint, including the read-only ones.

## Conventions
- Success envelope: `{ "data": ..., "meta": { ... } }`.
- Error envelope: `{ "error": { "code", "message", "details" } }`.
- Monetary values (`price_excl_vat`, `vat_rate`) are sent as decimal **strings**, never floats, to preserve precision.
- Creating a product captures the new id into the `product_id` collection variable, so Update/Delete work right after.

item.name: Products
item.description: CRUD operations on persisted products. All routes require the Bearer token.

exec: // Capture the new product id so Update/Delete requests work out of the box.

request.description:
Create a product. Returns **201 Created** with the created product (incl. computed `price_with_vat`) and a `Location` header.

Required fields: `name`, `brand`, `category`, `price_excl_vat`, `vat_rate`. `description` is optional.

The response id is stored in the `product_id` collection variable.

query.description: Filter by exact category. Empty/whitespace = no filter. Disable to skip.
Filter by exact brand. Empty/whitespace = no filter. Disable to skip.
Page number (>= 1). Non-numeric/out-of-range falls back to 1.
Items per page (1..100). Defaults to 20; clamped to a max of 100.

request.description:
List products with all available options at once: `category`, `brand`, `page`, `per_page`. Disable any query param (uncheck it) to drop that filter — with all disabled you get the default listing (page 1, 20 per page).

Returns `{ data: [...], meta: { page, per_page, total, total_pages } }`.

Partial update — only the supplied fields are validated and changed, so you can send any subset (e.g. just `{ "price_excl_vat": "17.50" }`). Uses the `product_id` captured by the Create request. Returns **200 OK** with the updated product, **404** if it does not exist.
Delete the product identified by `product_id`. Returns **204 No Content** on success, **404** if it does not exist.

item.name: Sample / Test data
item.description: Read-only sample products from a configurable source. Nothing is persisted — copy a sample into the Create request to store it.

query.description: Read samples from the local JSON dataset shipped with the app.
Fetch samples from the external DummyJSON API (https://dummyjson.com). Requires outbound internet access from the PHP container.

request.description: Sample products fetched live from the **DummyJSON** external API and mapped to the product shape.
